### Motivation and Context

<!--
  Please describe the motivation behind this change and how it fits into the overall project. You can also include any relevant background information or context that may help reviewers understand the purpose of this change.
-->

### Influenced Characters

<!--
  Please list the characters that are influenced by this change. You can use the following format:
  - U+XXXX: <character> (name)
-->

### Glyph Quantity

<!--
  Please provide the number of new glyphs introduced by this change. If the change involves modifying existing glyphs, please specify the number of modified glyphs as well. If you do not have accurate numbers, please provide an estimate.
-->

### Samples

<!--
  Please provide sample images of the influenced characters before and after the change. You can use the following format:
  - Before: ![before](link_to_before_image)
  - After: ![after](link_to_after_image)
-->

### Checklist

- [ ] Variant shape **does not go too far away from Iosevka's design**.
- [ ] The variant **does not conflict with any characters in Unicode that Iosevka currently supports**.
- [ ] This variant is added to make one or multiple stylistic set to match the corresponded font better
- [ ] This variant has been seen in at least two different monospace/programming fonts created by different designers. <!-- Please provide details here. -->
